findings.claim: `render_binary_operand` conservatively parenthesises any infix operand, so nested same-precedence terms over-parenthesise (`a + b + c` → `(a + b) + c`, and `mpg ~ wt + cyl + hp` → `mpg ~ (wt + cyl) + hp`); it is package-wide and semantically safe (formula `terms()` identical), not a formula-specific bug.
findings.detail: Confirmed via `terms()` equivalence on the rendered vs source formula. A precedence-aware renderer would remove it, but it changes no semantics.
findings.verification: render_binary_operand (render.R:337) parenthesises any operand that is itself a binary infix call (340), producing (a+b)+c nesting; confirmed terms()-identical this session. Source ref line-drifted (314-332 -> ~337).

findings.claim: `ggplot()`'s `data =` argument is independently run through `try_lift_to_pipeline`, so a computed data source such as `broom::augment(lm(...))` is subject to the same canonical-pipeline lift (and its formula-tearing) as a top-level `|>` chain.
findings.detail: This is why the bug manifested in data-source position, not just in surface `|>` pipelines — the lift fires on the data_arg subtree too.
findings.verification: classify_walk.ptr_layer (classify.R:40-42) classifies node$data_arg; ptr_classify_calls calls try_lift_to_pipeline (translate.R:178). A computed data_arg is therefore subject to the lift.

findings.claim: The descent's placeholder/formula atomicity guard must test `cur` (the node we have descended TO), NOT `cur[[2L]]` — guarding on `cur[[2L]]` (stop before descending) breaks a legitimate bottom source like `ppUpload(d)` and makes its pipeline fail to lift.
findings.detail: A source-role placeholder at the pipeline head (`ppUpload(d) |> filter(...)`) must still be REACHED as `cur` and accepted; `!is_placeholder_call(cur)` stops decomposing a placeholder's own args while still letting the placeholder be the source. Empirically, `!is_placeholder_call(cur[[2L]])` rendered `lm()` empty and dropped the upstream pipeline; `!is_placeholder_call(cur)` was correct.
findings.verification: Current while-loop (lines 267-268) and post-loop (288) both guard on the descended-to node `cur` via !is_placeholder_call(cur) && !is_formula_call(cur); the cur[[2L]] failure was an empirical session result.

findings.claim: The plot is evaluated by reconstructing language objects from the typed tree (`layer_to_eval_expr`/`node_to_lang`), entirely independent of `ptr_render()`'s text output, so a broken/unparseable code panel can coexist with a correct rendered plot.
findings.detail: The doubled-paren render bug produced unparseable code_text yet `ptr_assemble_plot_safe` still evaluated correctly — proof the two paths are decoupled. Implication: render-only defects are display bugs, not plot-correctness bugs; reproduce them via code_text, not ggplot_build.
findings.source: R/paintr-eval.R (ptr_eval, layer_to_eval_expr, node_to_lang); R/paintr-render.R (render_walk); R/paintr-safe.R (ptr_complete_expr_safe vs ptr_assemble_plot_safe)
findings.verification: ptr_eval (eval.R:9) maps layers via layer_to_eval_expr (27) -> node_to_lang (107) and evals those (23); independent of render_walk. Decoupling confirmed.

findings.claim: Treating `~` as atomic in the LIFT (resugar) does not bury placeholders inside a formula, because `translate_call()` independently recurses into every argument — so `lm(ppVar(y) ~ x)` still detects and wires the `ppVar` widget.
findings.detail: The lift (try_lift/resugar, pure AST, pre-translate) and the node tree build (translate_node/translate_call) are separate passes; the formula-atomicity guard lives only in the lift, leaving placeholder detection intact.
findings.source: R/paintr-translate.R:641-700 (translate_node, translate_call); resugar guards are lift-only
findings.verification: translate_node (translate.R:641) returns translate_call(expr) for calls (681); translate_call recurses translate_node over every arg. resugar guards live only in resugar_pipeline_stages (lift), so placeholder detection in formulas is intact.

findings.claim: `try_lift_to_pipeline` requires `identical(rebuild_nested_from_stages(parts), canonical)` before lifting, so a mis-shaped split can never emit a corrupt pipeline — the worst case is the lift declines and the expression falls back to a plain nested call.
findings.detail: This bounds the blast radius of any change to the descent/gates: tightening them can only make the lift MORE conservative, never produce a broken tree.
findings.verification: try_lift_to_pipeline at lines 418-420 returns round-trip-mismatch unless identical(rebuilt, canonical), confirming the safety-net property.

findings.claim: A canonical-pipeline source slot must be a bare symbol or a SOURCE-role placeholder only; the grounding gate uses `is_data_source_placeholder_call()` (entry$role == "source") rather than `is_placeholder_call()` (any non-structural role), so a value/consumer placeholder (`ppExpr`/`ppVar`) in source position is rejected → opaque-call fallback.
findings.detail: Before the fix the gate accepted ANY placeholder call as a source, which let `ppExpr(formula)` be lifted as the data frame. `is_placeholder_call` (atomicity, all roles) is still used in the descent loop; only source ACCEPTANCE was narrowed to source-role.
findings.verification: Re-verified @ HEAD: trigger was the unrelated paintr-translate.R comment swap; is_data_source_placeholder_call (entry role == source) at R/paintr-translate.R:369 still gates the canonical-pipeline source slot. Claim holds.

findings.claim: Built-in placeholder registry roles are: ppUpload=`source`, ppExpr/ppNum/ppText=`value`, ppVar=`consumer`, ppLayerOff/ppVerbSwitch=`structural`; a custom source registered via `ptr_define_placeholder_source()` also gets role `source`.
findings.detail: The role tokens "source"/"value"/"consumer" are set programmatically (not as grep-able string literals in the registry source), so they must be read from the live registry, not a static grep. Verified by enumerating ptr_registry_lookup() at runtime and registering a custom source.
findings.source: R/paintr-builtins.R:332-374 (ptr_register_builtins via ptr_define_placeholder_*); verified live via ptr_registry_lookup()
findings.verification: Re-verified @ HEAD 29571ac80afa. ptr_register_builtins (R/paintr-builtins.R:310-393) wires the same keyword->role mappings the finding claims: ppText (332), ppNum (342), ppExpr (349) via ptr_define_placeholder_value; ppVar (356) via ptr_define_placeholder_consumer; ppUpload (367) via ptr_define_placeholder_source; ppLayerOff/ppVerbSwitch (380-381) via ptr_register_structural_keyword. The three public constructors fix the role positionally in their ptr_define_core() call: value (R/paintr-registry.R:617 'value'), consumer (740 'consumer'), source (899 'source'). ptr_register_structural_keyword (924) hard-sets entry$role='structural' (939). A custom source via ptr_define_placeholder_source() therefore also gets role='source'. The detail (roles set programmatically by the wrapper, not grep-able string literals at the registration sites) still holds. The ADR 0027 rename that triggered the stale flag changed only ARGUMENT names inside these calls (validate_input->validate_session_input at builtins.R:360, positional_arg->parse_positional_arg at 336/346/353/361/372, ptr_arg_string_vector->ptr_arg_string at 336) — the finding references none of those arg names; it asserts only keyword->role mappings, which are unchanged. Claim and detail both hold; kill test passes (role tokens are assigned programmatically and are not grep-able literals, so a fresh session still saves the live-registry-enumeration discovery effort).

findings.claim: A ppExpr value resolves via `ptr_builtin_expr_resolve_expr` (rlang::parse_exprs of the typed text) into a `ptr_user_expr` node rendered through `expr_text`/deparse — a DIFFERENT render path than the `translate_call` ptr_call used for a bare (non-ppExpr) formula.
findings.detail: This is why a ppExpr-held formula rendered infix correctly even before the binary-`~` render fix, while a bare `lm(y ~ x)` formula (translate_call → ptr_call) needed it. The two paths diverge for the same surface syntax.
findings.verification: substitute_walk.ptr_ph_value returns ptr_user_expr(resolved) for ppExpr (substitute.R:125-126); ptr_builtin_expr_resolve_expr (builtins.R:139) uses rlang::parse_exprs (157). Distinct path from translate_call.

findings.claim: `render_walk.ptr_call` renders a 2-arg call infix only when `is_binary_infix_op(name)` is true, and that list omitted `~`, so a two-sided formula `gear ~ cyl` fell through to prefix `~(gear, cyl)` while one-sided `~cyl` rendered fine via the separate unary branch (`name %in% c("-","+","!","~")`).
findings.detail: Fixed with a dedicated two-sided `~` branch that renders operands with plain `render_walk` (no conservative parenthesisation) because `~` is R's lowest-precedence operator and its operands never need wrapping — yielding clean `y ~ a + b`.
findings.verification: is_binary_infix_op (render.R:326) still omits the tilde; a dedicated two-sided tilde branch exists at line 276 (name=='~'); unary tilde handled separately. Matches the finding (fix via dedicated branch, not list addition).

findings.claim: An explicit parenthesis AST node `(x)` (head is the `(` symbol) hit the generic call renderer, which emitted the head `(` plus its own arg-parens → `((x)` (unbalanced, unparseable); a dedicated 1-arg `(` branch emitting `(inner)` is required.
findings.detail: Pre-existing and general — affected every parenthesised sub-expression (`geom_point(size=(2+1)*2)`, `filter((a+b)>1)`, `aes(x=(a+b)*c)`), not just formulas. Eval was unaffected (typed tree), so it was a code-panel-only defect until formulas (which lean on parens, e.g. `a * (b + c)`) made it user-visible. Parens are load-bearing in model formulas, so this is correctness-adjacent.
findings.verification: A dedicated 1-arg paren branch exists at render.R:291 (name=='('), confirming the prior generic fall-through emitted the doubled '((x)'. Fix present.

findings.claim: `resugar_pipeline_stages()`'s always-aggressive descent walks through ANY call whose first positional arg is itself a call — including a placeholder call's arg and a formula `~` call — so without explicit atomicity guards it mistakes an inner symbol (a formula LHS like `y`, a value placeholder) for the pipeline data source.
findings.detail: For `broom::augment(lm(ppExpr(y ~ x), data = ppUpload(d)))` the descent reached `ppExpr(...)` then `y ~ x` then lifted `y` as the source, producing the nonsense pipeline `y |> ~(x) |> lm() |> augment()`. The guard belongs in BOTH the while-loop and the post-loop split. ADR 0012 ("tree is semantic, not syntactic") deliberately removed verb whitelists, which is why the descent is this aggressive.
findings.verification: Confirmed: resugar_pipeline_stages at R/paintr-translate.R:264 with aggressive descent; guards added at lines 267-268 and 288 precisely because the descent walks through any call-headed first arg.

findings.claim: `~` is on the safe-operator allowlist (with `:`, `%in%`, `!`), NOT the denylist, so the formula bug was never a safety/parse rejection; base parsers (`parse`, `str2lang`, `rlang::parse_expr`) all handle a formula string fine.
findings.detail: Rules out the denylist/safety walker as the cause of formula breakage; `extract_fn_names`'s `str2lang` is tryCatch-guarded (returns character(0) on failure), so it cannot propagate a parse error either. The defect was purely structural (lift) + cosmetic (render).
findings.verification: CORRECTION: there is NO ptr_safe_operators symbol in the codebase. The tilde appears in pruneable_operator_names (utils.R:468-472), which governs PRUNING, not safety. The load-bearing CONCLUSION still holds: tilde is NOT in unsafe_expr_denylist (verified) and base parsers handle formula strings, so the formula bug was never a safety/denylist rejection. The finding's specific list-name/symbol is wrong; its conclusion is right.
